message:
So last week my teenage son left the stove on—like, actually on—while making nachos and then wandered off to his room. I smelled it before anything got scary, thank goodness. But the *funny* part? I texted the family group chat 'EVERYONE OUT' and got back three different emoji panic reactions before anyone had even moved. 😅

It made me realize we talk *about* fire safety all the time, but we'd never actually talked about what leaving the house actually looks like. Like, do we meet at the mailbox? The car? Does the dog come? (Yes, the dog comes.)

Since then we've sketched out a super basic escape plan—nothing fancy, just 'here's our meeting spot, here's why smoke alarms are non-negotiable.' Turns out when everyone knows the actual plan, the panic chat turns into a practiced habit instead.

Does your family have a fire escape route that everyone *actually* knows, or is it more of a 'we'll figure it out' situation?
